title: Spiral Matrix
prompt:
Given an `m x n` matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order.

Spiral order starts from the top-left corner, goes right across the top row, then down the right column, then left across the bottom row, then up the left column, and repeats inward.

examples.output:
[1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 8, 7, 4, 5]
Explanation: Start top-left, go right (1,2,3), down (6,9), left (8,7), up (4), right (5).

notesForAI.coreIdea: Maintain four boundaries: top, bottom, left, right. Traverse right along top row, down along right column, left along bottom row, up along left column. Shrink boundaries after each pass. Stop when boundaries cross.
commonBugs: Not checking if boundaries have crossed before the bottom or left traversal (causes duplicates in non-square matrices)
Not handling single-row or single-column matrices
